name: Cash & Debt Reconciliation
tagline: Deterministic cash & debt reconciliation, keyed by account number, materiality-gated.
plain_summary: The engine ties each entity's GL cash to the bank ending balance and each GL loan to a 3-part lender total (principal + interest/reserve + late paydown), matching by account number so a figure never lands on the wrong row. Differences at or under a $50 materiality threshold are logged as timing noise; anything larger becomes a numbered flag for review. Every run emits the same 5-section evidence log — a Markdown file and a color-coded Excel workbook. An optional assurance loop restores a drifted package back to the seeded source of record and applies a materiality gate before it ships.
marketing_thesis: A reconciliation is only as trustworthy as the evidence trail behind it. Every figure on this page re-derives from the public repository — the demo is a command you run, not a claim you take on faith.
layers_intro: Each layer is an independent stage, from the seeded substrate up to the optional assurance loop. Select a layer for its plain-terms and engineering detail.

layers.label: Assurance loop & materiality gate
layers.plain: Optional watchdog: when the stored package drifts from source, it restores the drifted accounts one at a time and issues a PASS, FLAG, or FAIL verdict before anything ships.
layers.engineering: observe -> detect -> remediate -> re-verify -> gate. A deviation sensor fingerprints each account against the baseline reconciliation of the pristine seed; resync_account restores every drifted GL/bank/lender/dormant field. Verdict: FAIL (not converged, exit 1), FLAG (converged, corrections above materiality, exit 0), PASS (converged, immaterial, exit 0).

layers.label: CLI orchestration & exit contract
layers.plain: The command you actually run: it wires the pieces together, prints the summary, and exits 0 because a flag is an expected result, not a crash.
layers.engineering: argparse flags --threshold / --seed / --no-xlsx / --markdown-path / --xlsx-path call generate_dataset -> reconcile -> render_markdown / write_xlsx, then return 0 even when lines are flagged. run.py and __main__.py are thin aliases.

layers.label: Evidence-log rendering
layers.plain: Turns the result into the same 5-section evidence log every time: a Markdown file and a color-coded Excel workbook.
layers.engineering: render_markdown emits Summary / Cash / Debt / Flagged / Notes; write_xlsx builds a 5-sheet openpyxl workbook with green/yellow/red/grey status fills and accounting-style number formatting.

layers.label: Reconciliation core
layers.plain: Ties each account to its source by account number, computes the difference, and labels it clean, timing, or flagged.
layers.engineering: compute_variance = round(gl - source, 2); lender_three_part_total = principal + current_interest_reserve + late_paydown; classify against a $0.005 tie tolerance and the materiality threshold; account-number indexing (never row position); dormant/zero-activity skip; sequential FLAG-### ids across cash then debt.

layers.label: Seeded synthetic substrate
layers.plain: Builds a repeatable set of books — GL, bank, and lender statements for four fictional entities — with known problems baked in to prove the engine catches them.
layers.engineering: random.Random(seed) yields byte-identical GL/bank/lender workpapers and injects seeded discrepancies: a deposit-in-transit, a sub-threshold timing accrual, an outright keying error, a populated late-paydown leg (clean tie), and a dormant zero-activity account.

blocks.label: Seeded generator
blocks.plain: Builds the fictional GL, bank, and lender books with known discrepancies injected.

blocks.label: Scope & account-number index
blocks.plain: Filters out dormant zero-activity rows and indexes each source by account number.

blocks.label: Variance + 3-part debt total
blocks.plain: Computes GL-minus-source for cash, and GL vs the 3-part lender total for debt.
blocks.engineering: _reconcile_cash vs bank.ending_balance; _reconcile_debt vs lender_three_part_total; compute_variance rounds to cents.

blocks.label: Materiality classify + flag ids
blocks.plain: Labels each line clean, timing, or flagged and numbers the flags.
blocks.engineering: classify(): |var| <= 0.005 clean, <= threshold timing, else flag; _assign_flag_ids -> FLAG-001.. across cash then debt.

blocks.label: 5-section evidence log
blocks.plain: Renders Summary / Cash / Debt / Flagged / Notes as Markdown and a color-coded xlsx.

blocks.label: CLI summary + exit 0
blocks.plain: Prints the run summary and flagged list; exits 0 even with flags.
blocks.engineering: cli.main prints period / materiality / counts / flagged and returns 0 (flags are expected, not failures).

blocks.label: Baseline reconcile (source of record)
blocks.plain: Reconciles the pristine seeded dataset once to establish the authoritative outcomes.
blocks.engineering: assurance_loop: pristine = generate_dataset(seed); baseline = reconcile(pristine); budget = len(gl_records) + 2.

blocks.label: Deviation sensor
blocks.plain: Compares each account's current outcome to the baseline and lists what drifted.
blocks.engineering: _outcomes() fingerprint (classification + variance + source_label), then deviations(current, baseline) sorted by account.

blocks.label: Resync to source
blocks.plain: Restores every record the lowest-numbered deviating account owns back to the seeded truth.
blocks.engineering: resync_account -> _resync_records for gl/bank/lender; books each field change as an Adjustment; removes or restores whole records.

blocks.label: Materiality gate -> verdict + exit code
blocks.plain: Once nothing deviates, it decides PASS / FLAG / FAIL and sets the process exit code.
blocks.engineering: total = sum(adjustment magnitudes); not converged -> FAIL (exit 1); total > materiality -> FLAG (exit 0); else PASS (exit 0). verdict_exit_code().

spec_strip.label: Materiality threshold
spec_strip.plain: differences at or under $50 are timing noise; above becomes a numbered flag

spec_strip.label: Accounts in scope
spec_strip.source: python -m recon_engine console summary; evidence-log.md summary section
spec_strip.plain: the fixed seeded scenario across four fictional entities

spec_strip.label: Tie tolerance
spec_strip.plain: absolute variance at or below this is an exact tie; guards float noise

spec_strip.label: Tests
spec_strip.source: python -m pytest --collect-only -q (cash-reconciliation/); verified 2026-07-20 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json
spec_strip.plain: the checks that pin this engine's behavior

spec_strip.label: Dependencies
spec_strip.plain: no pandas / numpy / faker; runs offline and deterministic

problem_statement: Reconciling cash and debt means tying every bank and lender movement to the general ledger, explaining the differences, and keeping audit-ready evidence across many entities. Performed by hand the work is repetitive and slow, and the evidence trail comes out inconsistent from month to month. This engine automates the tie-out on a repeatable cadence, builds materiality and escalation into the process so reviewers see signal instead of noise, and produces a consistent, audit-ready evidence log every cycle.

scenarios.title: March 2026 base reconciliation
scenarios.narrative: python -m recon_engine reconciles the seeded March-2026 books: 9 accounts (4 cash, 4 debt, 1 dormant skipped) resolve to 5 clean, 1 timing, 2 flagged. FLAG-001 CASH-1001 (Maple Fund LP) is a -1,875.40 deposit-in-transit; FLAG-002 DEBT-2003 (Cedar Ridge Partners LLC) is a +12,500.00 keying error. The process exits 0.
scenarios.source: README.md 'Real example output'; evidence-log.md summary and flagged sections

scenarios.title: The 3-part debt formula ties out
scenarios.narrative: The debt section proves the optional third leg is wired through. DEBT-2000 (Demo Holdings LLC) carries a 7,500.00 late-paydown mirrored GL-to-lender and still ties clean at 0.00; DEBT-2002 (Birchwood Op Co) shows an 18.75 interest accrual booked in GL but not yet on the lender statement, which is under $50 and therefore a yellow timing item, not a flag.
scenarios.source: README.md debt example table; recon_engine/generate.py (injected late-paydown and timing difference)

scenarios.title: The assurance loop restores a drifted package
scenarios.narrative: python -m recon_engine.loop --demo injects three faults on baseline-clean accounts: CASH-1002 GL fat-fingered +4,830.25, DEBT-2000 lender statement dropped, DEBT-2001 mis-marked dormant. The loop resyncs those three accounts in three turns (budget 11), driving deviations from 3 to 0. Gross corrective movement of 4,831.25 exceeds the $50 materiality, so the verdict is FLAG with exit 0, and the scenario's genuine flags are left untouched.

instruction_set.output: console summary: period, seed, materiality, in-scope counts, clean/timing/flagged, and the flagged list

instruction_set.operation: convenience alias for python -m recon_engine (same args)

instruction_set.output: borderline variances reclassify between timing and flag

instruction_set.operation: regenerate synthetic data with a different seed
instruction_set.output: a new byte-deterministic dataset and log
console summary without the XLSX line

instruction_set.operation: inject the drift profile and run the assurance loop
instruction_set.output: turn-by-turn Markdown; verdict PASS/FLAG/FAIL with gross movement vs materiality
instruction_set.artifacts: assurance_loop_report.md when --out DIR is given; otherwise stdout only

instruction_set.operation: run the default test suite (the SWEEP-gated bulk grid is excluded)

benchmarks.source: python -m pytest --collect-only -q in cash-reconciliation/ (measured, SWEEP unset); pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json
benchmarks.plain: an exhaustive bulk invariant grid raises this to 162,479 with SWEEP=1

benchmarks.label: Seeded reconciliation
benchmarks.plain: 5 clean, 1 timing, 2 flagged in the March-2026 seed

benchmarks.label: Loop convergence
benchmarks.plain: restores three injected faults to the source of record

benchmarks.label: Loop gross correction
benchmarks.source: python -m recon_engine.loop --demo (measured: 4,830.25 GL fat-finger + 1.00 dormant-flag restore)
benchmarks.plain: material, so the verdict is FLAG (converged, exit 0) rather than PASS

verdict_map.action: comment as timing/noise, expected to clear; not escalated
assign a sequential flag id; escalate for review
skip with a documented note; no reconciliation required
ship with a note of what moved; exit 0

verdict_map.severity: loop: not converged within the turn budget

guarantees: Deterministic: random.Random(seed) yields byte-identical GL/bank/lender data, so a given seed always reproduces the same log (generate.py; --seed).
Account-number targeting: every value is resolved by account number in the key column, never by row index, which defeats off-by-one corruption (engine._index_banks / _index_lenders).

Integer-cent discipline: all money is round(..., 2) at every step (compute_variance, lender_three_part_total, ReconLine fields), with a $0.005 tie tolerance guarding float noise (engine.TIE_TOLERANCE).
Materiality gate: |variance| <= threshold is timing/noise; above becomes a numbered FLAG-### escalation (engine.classify, _assign_flag_ids).
3-part debt formula: the GL loan is reconciled against principal + current_interest_reserve + late_paydown, the optional third leg included (engine.lender_three_part_total).
Loop fidelity: the assurance loop never invents a number; every correction restores a seeded record, and the settled reconciliation is identical to a pristine run (loop.py).
Exit contract: the base engine returns 0 even when lines are flagged, since a flag is an expected outcome; the loop returns 1 only on non-convergence (cli.main, loop.verdict_exit_code).

gate_policy.note: The base engine always exits 0 — a flag is an expected reconciliation outcome, not a program failure. The assurance loop adds an automated materiality gate: PASS and FLAG both exit 0 (FLAG surfaces material corrections so a reviewer sees what moved before the package ships), while FAIL exits 1 only on non-convergence. The gate labels and routes the output; it does not stand in for a human sign-off.

limits.statement: It refuses to force a tie on dormant, zero-activity accounts: they are skipped with a documented note rather than reconciled, keeping padding out of the population.

The assurance loop refuses to converge by zeroing genuine reconciling items: the scenario's real flags (deposit-in-transit, keying error) are part of the baseline and survive the loop; it restores fidelity to source, not 'all clean'.
limits.source: recon_engine/loop.py (docstring; demo_setup targets only baseline-clean accounts)

limits.statement: The loop refuses to invent a number: every correction is a restoration of a seeded source record, so the settled reconciliation is byte-identical to a pristine run.
limits.source: recon_engine/loop.py (resync_account; fields are only ever set back to pristine truth)

media.poster_alt: Animated overview of the Cash & Debt Reconciliation engine: seeded GL, bank, and lender books tie out by account number, with lines resolving to clean, timing, and flagged and a materiality gate on the differences.
media.caption: Animated overview of the recon run on fictional seeded data; run the commands above to reproduce.

meta.description: Cash & Debt Reconciliation (SFS-E02-RCN) datasheet — a deterministic engine that ties GL cash to bank ending balances and GL debt to a 3-part lender total by account number, materiality-gates the differences into clean/timing/flagged, and emits a 5-section evidence log; every figure re-derives from the public repository.
